revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''倭の六県'''とは、[[大和国]]にあった[[大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地のことである。 [[645年]]([[大化]]元年)、8月5日…」
revision.text:
'''倭の六県'''とは、[[大和国]]にあった[[大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地のことである。
[[645年]]([[大化]]元年)、[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（戸籍作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>日本書紀巻二十五、孝徳天皇大化元年条</ref>。「六県」は「[[曾布]]（そふ）・[[山辺]]（やまのべ）・[[磯城]]（しき）・[[十市]]（とおち）・[[高市]]（たけち）・[[葛城]]（かつらぎ）」を指すもので、「延喜式」の祈年祭の祝詞に、上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上した、とある。奈良盆地の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
域内に、[[山辺御県坐神社]]・[[葛木御県神社]]などの式内社がある。
== 参考資料 == * 「角川第二版日本史辞典」、高柳光寿・竹内理三：編、1966年、角川書店 * 「岩波日本史辞典」、監修：永原慶二、編集委員：石上英二、加藤哲郎、工楽善通、五味利彦、高埜利彦、西成田豊、藤井譲治、油井正臣、和田晴吾、1999年、岩波書店
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[県]] * [[県主]] * [[奈良県]] * [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]]

contributor.username: ja>壁掛成蛸は三考する葦である

'''倭の六県'''（やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。「六県」は「曽布（そふ）・山辺（やまのべ）・磯城（しき）・十市（とおち）・高市（たけち）・[[葛城]]（かつらぎ）」を指すもので、『[[延喜式]]』の[[祈年祭]]の祝詞には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上した、とある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
域内に、[[山辺御県坐神社]]・[[葛木御県神社]]などの式内社がある。

revision.comment: 蘇我馬子エピソード追加
revision.text:
'''倭の六県'''（やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。「六県」は「曽布〔添〕（そう）・山辺（やまのべ）・磯城（しき）・十市（とおち）・高市（たけち）・[[葛城]]（かつらぎ）」を指すもので、『[[延喜式]]』の[[祈年祭]]の祝詞には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上した、とある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)に、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、臣の姓の名前もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我家の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年11月条</ref>。。
域内に、[[山辺御県坐神社]]・[[葛木御県神社]]などの式内社がある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1966年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[県]] * [[県主]] * [[奈良県]] * [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]]

'''倭の六県'''（やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。「六県」は「曽布(添)（そう）・[[山辺郡|山辺]]（やまのべ）・[[磯城]]（しき）・[[十市郡|十市]]（とおち）・[[高市郡|高市]]（たけち）・[[葛城]]（かつらぎ）」を指すもので、『[[延喜式]]』の[[祈年祭]]の祝詞には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上したとある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、(蘇我一族の「[[臣]](おみ)」という)「[[カバネ|姓]]名(かばねな)」もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我氏の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年10月条</ref>。
域内に、[[山辺御県坐神社]]・[[葛木御県神社]]などの式内社がある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1974年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|県]] * [[県主]] * [[奈良県]] * [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]] * [[弟磯城]]

'''倭の六県'''（'''六御縣'''、やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。「六県」は「曽布(添)（そう）・[[山辺郡|山辺]]（やまのべ）・[[磯城]]（しき）・[[十市郡|十市]]（とおち）・[[高市郡|高市]]（たけち）・[[葛城]]（かつらぎ）」を指すもので、『[[延喜式]]』の[[祈年祭]]の祝詞には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上したとある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、(蘇我一族の「[[臣]](おみ)」という)「[[カバネ|姓]]名(かばねな)」もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我氏の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年10月条</ref>。
域内に、[[山辺御県坐神社]]・[[葛木御県神社]]などの式内社がある。

== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1974年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|県]] * [[県主]] * [[奈良県]] * [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]] * [[弟磯城]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとのむつのあかた}} [[Category:かつて存在した日本の行政区分]] [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:奈良時代]] [[Category:平安時代の政治]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:名数6]]

'''倭の六県'''（'''六御縣'''、やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。 「六県」は『[[和名類聚抄]]』での以下の各郡域に相当する。
* 曽布(添)県 （そふ） - [[添上郡]]、[[添下郡]] * 山辺県（やまのべ） - [[山辺郡]] * [[磯城]]県（しき、志貴） - [[式上郡]]、[[式下郡]] * 十市県（とおち） - [[十市郡]] * 高市県（たけち） - [[高市郡]] * [[葛城]]県（かつらぎ、葛木） - [[葛上郡]]、[[葛下郡]]
域内には県名を冠する[[添御県坐神社]]（[[奈良市]]三碓、奈良市歌姫町）、[[山邊御県坐神社]]（[[天理市]]別所町、天理市西井戸堂町）、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（[[桜井市]]三輪金屋）、[[十市御縣坐神社]]（[[橿原市]]十市町）、[[高市御県坐神社]]（橿原市四条町）、[[葛木御県神社]]（[[葛城市]]葛木）の式内社が置かれている。
『[[延喜式]]』にある27篇の[[祝詞]]のうち[[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上したとある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、(蘇我一族の「[[臣]](おみ)」という)「[[カバネ|姓]]名(かばねな)」もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我氏の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年10月条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1974年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|県]] * [[県主]] * [[奈良県]] * [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]] * [[弟磯城]]

『[[延喜式]]』にある27篇の[[祝詞]]のうち[[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上したとある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、(蘇我一族の「[[臣]](おみ)」という)「[[カバネ|姓]]名(かばねな)」もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我氏の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年10月条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1974年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|県]] - [[県主]]（[[弟磯城]]） * [[奈良県]] - [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]]

revision.text: * * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:倭の六県|The Six Prefectures of Wa]] * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:葛城|Katsuragi]] * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:大山守皇子|Prince Oyamamori]] * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:菟道稚郎子|Ujichi Wakako]] * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:住吉仲皇子|Prince Sumiyoshi]] * {{ill|10=ja|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:阿曇浜子|Azumi Hamako]] * {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|fr]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]]

[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:倭の六県]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:葛城]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:大山守皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:菟道稚郎子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:住吉仲皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:阿曇浜子]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]]

[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:倭の六県]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:葛城]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:大山守皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:菟道稚郎子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:住吉仲皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-The Six Prefectures of Wa-qq]] [[ja:阿曇浜子]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]] '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624年}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} * {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン}}

However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.

revision.text:
[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:倭の六県]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:葛城]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:大山守皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:菟道稚郎子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:住吉仲皇子]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[category:qq close]] [[ja:阿曇浜子]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]] '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624年}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} * {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Former Japanese administrative divisions]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Heian period politics]] [[Category:History of Kashihara City]] [[Category:Japanese numerical lists]] [[Category:bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]][[sn:The Six Prefectures of Wa]][[Category:bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]]

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624年}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.

== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.

revision.text:
[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] <!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:倭の六県]]
<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:葛城]]
<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:大山守皇子]]
<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:菟道稚郎子]]
<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:住吉仲皇子]]
<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:阿曇浜子]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]] '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624年}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999.== See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} * {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン}}

<!--The Six Prefectures of Wa-->[[ja:阿曇浜子]] [[Category:Yamato no Agoko forks]] '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624th year }} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} * {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン}}

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名類聚抄|12=draft}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soegami District|ja|添上郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=draft}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡|12=draft}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=draft}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=draft}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takechi District|ja|高市郡|12=draft}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=draft}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=draft}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'', the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624th year|12=draft}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|12=draft}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|12=draft}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>

Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinai shrines|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=draft}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=draft}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=draft}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)

The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|ja|624th year|12=draft}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
16 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:倭の六県 up to rev 99762329
revision.text:
'''倭の六県'''（'''六御縣'''、やまとのむつのあがた）は、古代の[[大和国]]にあった[[治天下大王|大王]]（[[皇室]]）の直轄地である。
[[645年]]（[[大化]]元年）[[8月5日 (旧暦)|8月5日]]に、[[東国]]の[[国司]]とともに使者が派遣され、造籍（[[戸籍]]作成）・校田（田畝調査）が行われた<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十五、[[孝徳天皇]]大化元年条</ref>。 「六県」は『[[和名類聚抄]]』での以下の各郡域に相当する。
* 曽布(添)県 （そふ） - [[添上郡]]、[[添下郡]] * 山辺県（やまのべ） - [[山辺郡]] * [[磯城]]県（しき、志貴） - [[式上郡]]、[[式下郡]] * 十市県（とおち） - [[十市郡]] * 高市県（たけち） - [[高市郡]] * [[葛城]]県（かつらぎ、葛木） - [[葛上郡]]、[[葛下郡]]
域内には県名を冠する[[添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|添御県坐神社]]（[[奈良市]]三碓、奈良市歌姫町）、[[山邊御県坐神社]]（[[天理市]]別所町、天理市西井戸堂町）、[[志貴御県坐神社]]（[[桜井市]]三輪金屋）、[[十市御縣坐神社]]（[[橿原市]]十市町）、[[高市御県神社]]（橿原市四条町）、[[葛木御県神社]]（[[葛城市]]葛木）の式内社が置かれている。
『[[延喜式]]』にある27篇の[[祝詞]]のうち[[祈年祭]]（としごいのまつり）には上記の「六の御県（みあがた）」の名前が見え、蔬菜を献上したとある。六県は[[奈良盆地]]の全域を占めるが、広大なものではない。
[[624年]](推古天皇32年)、[[蘇我馬子]]は[[推古天皇]]に、県の一つである葛城県は蘇我氏の本居(うぶすな)で、(蘇我一族の「[[臣]](おみ)」という)「[[カバネ|姓]]名(かばねな)」もそこに由来するものだから、「封県」に賜りたいと懇願したが、蘇我氏の血をひく女帝から拒絶された、ともある<ref>『[[日本書紀]]』巻二十二、[[推古天皇]]32年10月条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考資料 == * [[高柳光寿]]・[[竹内理三]]編『角川第二版日本史辞典』角川書店、1974年 * [[永原慶二]]監修『岩波日本史辞典』岩波書店、1999年
== 関連項目 == * [[古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|県]] - [[県主]]（[[弟磯城]]） * [[奈良県]] - [[橿原市]] * [[平城・相楽ニュータウン]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまとのむつのあかた}} [[Category:かつて存在した日本の行政区分]] [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:奈良時代]] [[Category:平安時代の政治]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:日本の名数6]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55521849]] Wikidata name: Six Agata in Yamato [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great King|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大王 (ヤマト王権)|1=Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}} (imperial family) in ancient [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|qq=|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|qq=|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|qq=|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|qq=|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|qq=|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|qq=|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|qq=|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] * [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]] * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-Sōraku New Town|qq=|1=Heijō-Sōraku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482302}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|倭の六県|version=99762329|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521849]] Wikidata name: Six Agata in Yamato [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大王 (ヤマト王権)|1=Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}} (imperial family) in ancient [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] * [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara City]] * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-Sōraku New Town|1=Heijō-Sōraku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482302}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|倭の六県|version=99762329|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521849]] Wikidata name: Six Agata in Yamato [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大王 (ヤマト王権)|1=Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}} (imperial family) in ancient [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
