On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', Volume 25, entry on [[:en:Emperor Kōtoku|Emperor Kōtoku]], Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''[[:en:Wamyō Ruijushō|Wamyō Ruijushō]]'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - [[:en:Soekami District, Nara|Soegami District]], {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]] * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - [[:en:Takaichi District, Nara|Takechi District]] * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], but their extent was not vast.
In [[:en:624|624]] (32nd year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]]), [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|WD=Q7553491|lt=Soegami District}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takechi District}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>

On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|WD=Q7553491|lt=Soegami District}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takechi District}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-Sōraku New Town|1=Heijō-Sōraku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482302}}

Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.

The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|WD=Q7553491|lt=Soegami District}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takechi District}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). '''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大王 (ヤマト王権)|1=Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=5th day of the 8th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|WD=Q7553491|lt=Soegami District}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takechi District}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Soe Mikenoza Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-Sōraku New Town|1=Heijō-Sōraku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482302}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:倭の六県]]
'''Wa no Mutsu Agata''' (倭の六県, also known as '''Roku no Miagata''') refers to six directly governed territories of the {{ill|Great King|ja|治天下大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great King|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大王 (ヤマト王権)|1=Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}} (imperial family) in ancient {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
On {{ill|August 5, 645|ja|8月5日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 5, 645|1=August 5 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840100}} (first year of the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika|1=Taika (era)}} era), messengers were dispatched alongside provincial officials in the eastern provinces to conduct household registration and land surveys.<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', Volume 25, entry on {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|en|Emperor Kōtoku|WD=Q317980|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}, Taika 1st Year.</ref>
The "Six Counties" correspond to the following districts as listed in ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'':
* '''Sofu (Soe) County''' (曽布・添県) - {{ill|Soekami District, Nara|en|Soekami District, Nara|WD=Q7553491|lt=Soegami District|1=Soekami District, Nara}}, {{ill|Soeshimo District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soeshimo District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}} * '''Yamanobe County''' (山辺県) - {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District|1=Yamabe District, Nara}} * '''Shiki County''' (磯城県) - {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|式上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|1=Shikijō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486985}}, {{ill|Shikige District|ja|式下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikige District|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}} * '''Tōchi County''' (十市県) - {{ill|Tōchi District|ja|十市郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi District|1=Toichi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405036}} * '''Takechi County''' (高市県) - {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|WD=Q1117901|lt=Takechi District|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} * '''Katsuragi County''' (葛城県) - {{ill|Katsujō District|ja|葛上郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsujō District|1=Katsujō district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620858}}, {{ill|Katsuge District|ja|葛下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuge District|1=Katsuge district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620862}}
Within these areas, several shrines bearing the name of their respective counties were established as {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, including: * {{ill|Soe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soe Mikenoza Shrine|1=Q45075623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45075623}} (Nara City, Miutsuka and Utahime) * {{ill|Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja|山邊御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tenri City, Bessho and Nishiidodō) * {{ill|Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja|志貴御県坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 志貴御縣坐神社|1=Shiki no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q63981812}} (Sakurai City, Miwa Kanaya) * {{ill|Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|十市御縣坐神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405029}} (Kashihara City, Tōchi) * {{ill|Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|高市御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takechi Mikenoza Shrine|1=Takechino Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q43594646}} (Kashihara City, Shijō) * {{ill|Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja|葛木御県神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Mikenoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 葛城御県神社|1=Katsuragi Miagata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620903}} (Katsuragi City, Katsuragi)
Among the 27 prayers listed in the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (Harvest Prayer Festival) specifically mentions offerings from these six counties, primarily vegetables. The six counties occupied the entirety of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, but their extent was not vast.
In {{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624|1=624}} (32nd year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}), {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}} petitioned Empress Suiko to grant him Katsuragi County as a private estate, citing it as the ancestral homeland of the Soga clan and the origin of their noble title ("Omi"). However, the empress, despite being of Soga descent, rejected the request.<ref>''Nihon Shoki'', Volume 22, entry on Empress Suiko, 32nd Year, 10th Month.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * Takayanagi Mitsuhisa & Takeuchi Rizo (eds.), "Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary," Kadokawa Shoten, 1974. * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), "Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary," Iwanami Shoten, 1999. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制#地方官制のはじまり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} * {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City|1=Kashihara, Nara}} * {{ill|Heijō-Sōraku New Town|ja|平城・相楽ニュータウン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heijō-Sōraku New Town|1=Heijō-Sōraku New Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482302}}
